judgment.summary: Applied Optoelectronics shows strong revenue growth and high analyst growth estimates, but is currently unprofitable with negative margins and free cash flow, and carries significant debt.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (ending 2026-03-31), EPS was -0.07, missing the estimate of -0.05 by 44.8%. Next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth of 51.4% YoY indicates strong top-line expansion.
Analyst growth estimates for the current fiscal year are exceptionally high at 441.7%.
The PEG ratio of 0.78 suggests the stock may be undervalued relative to its expected earnings growth.
A current ratio of 3.83 and quick ratio of 2.85 indicate strong short-term liquidity.
negatives: Operating margin of -8.6% and profit margin of -8.5% show the company is currently unprofitable.
Return on equity of -6.1% and return on assets of -3.3% highlight poor profitability relative to shareholder equity and assets.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $-445.58M indicates significant cash burn.
Debt/Equity ratio of 25.36 is very high, suggesting substantial leverage.
The mean analyst target of $151.30 is below the current spot price of $165.26.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant recent volatility, with a notable 10% slump on May 18th attributed to profit-taking in AI optics stocks. This downturn followed news of substantial insider selling, with multiple filings indicating sales exceeding $12 million in total. While Mark Cuban has recently commented on the importance of foundational AI, the immediate news context is dominated by recent price weakness and insider transactions.
